test_cases.input: Sequence from a nature documentary showing elephant matriarch leading herd to a water source during drought. Research notes on drought severity, herd memory, elephant navigation. Formal, David Attenborough-style register. 100 words.
test_cases.notes: Commentary correctly subordinated to image — no description of what was visible. Research facts integrated naturally. Rhythm was suitable for the formal register. Sentence lengths were appropriate for spoken delivery.

test_cases.input: Archival sequence for a historical documentary about a 1970s urban renewal project. Pictures: demolition of a neighborhood, displaced residents, new housing blocks. Research: dates, official justifications, resident testimony. Measured, factual register. 150 words.
test_cases.notes: Archive picture context was well-managed — narration added period detail not visible in the footage. Resident testimony was incorporated without editorializing. Commentary Notes correctly identified two moments where silence was preferable.

test_cases.input: Mid-film sequence from a personal documentary. Subject is alone at the site of a childhood home, now demolished. Minimal research notes — emotional content only. Intimate first-person-adjacent register. 80 words.
test_cases.notes: Handled sparse research notes gracefully — wrote commentary that worked from emotional observation rather than fact. Correctly recommended in Commentary Notes that this sequence may need the subject's own voice rather than third-person narration.
